title: Fiery, Velvety Saudi-Arabian Wheat Dolmeh (in a stone bowl)
description: A savory, velvety Saudi-Arabian dish featuring dry white wine, served in a hot stone dolma bowl. Suitable for a sick day or as a comforting meal.
instructions: Rinse the wheat berries and soak them in water for at least 4 hours or overnight.
Drain and rinse the wheat berries, then transfer them to a large pot with the vegetable broth, white wine, butter, onion, garlic, turmeric, cumin, cayenne pepper, and salt.
Bring the mixture to a boil, then reduce the heat to low and simmer for 30 minutes, or until the wheat berries are tender.
Use an immersion blender to puree the mixture until smooth, or allow it to cool and blend in a blender.
Return the pureed mixture to the pot and simmer over low heat for an additional 10 minutes.
Season the dolmeh with salt to taste, then serve hot in a stone dolma bowl, garnished with chopped parsley and cilantro if desired.
Traditionally, the dolma bowl is heated in the oven before serving to create a hot, sizzling surface.
Carefully remove the hot dolma bowl from the oven and place it on a heat-resistant surface.
Spoon the warmed dolmeh into the dolma bowl and serve immediately.
